instruction: Enable Bluetooth service and make it persistent across reboot by enabling the bluetooth systemd unit. Also create ~/Desktop/bluetooth_status.txt with two lines: enabled=yes and active=yes.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu Desktop Guide - Turn Bluetooth on or off (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/bluetooth-turn-on-off.html.en)

parameters.command: echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S bash -c 'apt-get update >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; apt-get install -y bluez >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; systemctl disable bluetooth >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; systemctl stop bluetooth >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'; rm -f ~/Desktop/bluetooth_status.txt

result.command: en=$(systemctl is-enabled bluetooth 2>/dev/null || echo 'disabled'); ac=$(systemctl is-active bluetooth 2>/dev/null || echo 'inactive'); expected=$(printf 'enabled=yes\nactive=yes\n'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/bluetooth_status.txt 2>/dev/null); file_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && file_ok='true'; echo "enabled=$en"; echo "active=$ac"; echo "file_ok=$file_ok"
